articles.title: Hidden Representations Without Hidden Layers
articles.description: What can be inferred about a language model’s semantic structure when its hidden states, weights, and intermediate representations are inaccessible?

articles.title: ChatGPT's Zombie Survival Plan Falls Apart When You Ask This
articles.description: A video case study demonstrating how adversarial questioning exposes hidden assumptions in large language model reasoning. Using a zombie survival scenario, the analysis shows how fuel logistics, dependency testing, and iterative contradiction reveal which parts of ChatGPT's answers remain structurally robust after their supporting assumptions collapse.

articles.title: Mining Google Maps Reviews for Unmet Customer Demand
articles.description: A JupyterLab workflow for extracting Google Maps reviews, filtering for causally useful customer experience signals, and turning local competitor reviews into unmet-demand analysis.

articles.title: AI SEO Strategy: Why Your Creative Work Is Invisible
articles.description: This video case study shows how AI-era search changes the value of content. Instead of chasing keyword volume, the project uses semantic SEO, network analysis, suppressed and emerging nodes, and creative systems design to turn search results into a coherent creative operating system.

articles.title: From SEO Keywords to the Haunted Machine: How AI Search Became a Creative Operating System
articles.description: This video case study shows how AI-era search changes the value of content. Instead of chasing keyword volume, the project uses semantic SEO, network analysis, suppressed and emerging nodes, and creative systems design to turn search results into a coherent creative operating system.

articles.title: How Search and Recommendation Systems Actually Work
articles.description: Search engines, recommendation systems, and AI retrieval systems appear different on the surface. Underneath, they are solving the same problem: selecting a small amount of information from a much larger set of possibilities.

articles.title: Semantic SEO Is Not Content Optimisation: It Is Graph Positioning
articles.description: Most discussions of semantic SEO focus on improving content. Increasingly, the more useful question is where that content sits within the network of entities, relationships, and information that search systems use to retrieve and rank information.

articles.title: Python for NLP and Semantic SEO: A Practical Reference
articles.description: A plain-language companion to the SEO Python codebase, explaining how the workflow uses search-result collection, NLP text processing, keyphrase extraction, embeddings, network analysis, and association rule mining for semantic SEO research.

articles.title: Semantic SEO in Python: From Search Results to Original Content Strategy
articles.description: How Python can be used to collect search-result data, expose semantic structure, identify unresolved topic clusters, and develop original content strategy from evidence rather than imitation.

articles.title: SDA-3 tl;dr
articles.description: A short explanation of SDA-3 as a method for mapping LLM response structure without claiming access to hidden reasoning.

articles.title: Research pipeline
articles.description: A multi-stage constraint system that reconstructs, filters, and stress-tests a search-space to identify which semantic structures are stable enough to act on.

articles.title: Who Benefits Most From This System
articles.description: This analysis identifies the users who benefit most from a structural semantic mapping pipeline, focusing on low-authority operators, advanced SEO strategists, research-oriented content systems, and artists working in emerging conceptual spaces.

articles.title: Clustered Output (Dataset)
articles.description: This post contains the structured cluster data derived from Instagram image analysis. It is presented in full without interpretation.

articles.title: What the Data Actually Looks Like
articles.description: This post documents the intermediate data produced after scraping and initial structuring. It shows what is actually being analysed before any creative conclusions are formed.

articles.title: Summary of the Process, Conclusions, and Their Validity
articles.description: This post presents a GPT-based analysis and evaluation of the research process used to investigate horror, surrealist, and symbolic aesthetics, assessing its conclusions for validity and internal consistency.

articles.title: Research Process Used to Establish the Seer-Clown Archetype
articles.description: This post outlines the structured, iterative research process used to develop the Seer-Clown archetype, combining data-driven analysis, artistic intuition, and philosophical exploration.

articles.title: YouTube
articles.description: A central index for video work connected to the site, including long-form explanations, methodology demonstrations, and related visual essays.

articles.title: Zombie Survival by ChatGPT — Why the AI Lies (and How to Stop It)
articles.description: This video demonstrates how AI systems optimise for coherence rather than structural accuracy. SDA-3 reframes the model as a system to interrogate, forcing it to expose suppressed variables and recover constraint-compatible truth.

articles.title: SDA-3 protocol
articles.description: This post introduces SDA-3, a protocol for inferring the structure of an LLM’s embedding space through observable outputs, without relying on access to internal weights or hidden states.

articles.title: Structural Extraction Protocol
articles.description: A unified, procedural system for extracting structurally necessary logic from language model outputs through recursive constraint, adversarial interrogation, and collapse enforcement.

articles.title: Art Forms with High Audience Resonance
articles.description: This post documents a ChatGPT response generated during an analysis of datasets related to scraped SEO data.

articles.title: First Principles
articles.description: These are the first principles that guide my artwork. They emerged from my research into horror, psychology, symbolism, and audience response, and act as the underlying logic behind the work that appears on this site.

articles.title: GPT Misuse Is Not What People Think
articles.description: Most discussions about GPT misuse focus on malicious intent or careless users. In reality, misuse usually happens when people expect the model to do things it was never designed to do.

articles.title: Maximising Artistic Identity
articles.description: This post documents a ChatGPT response generated during an analysis of my artwork and related datasets. The goal of the exercise was to identify stylistic patterns and develop a clearer artistic direction.

articles.title: Most Popular Art Mediums Based on the Datasets
articles.description: This post documents a ChatGPT response generated during an analysis of statistical outputs from my SEO research process. The goal of the exercise was to identify which artistic mediums appear most frequently in audience-facing datasets and how those mediums might inform future production methods.

articles.title: Semantic SEO Begins Before Optimisation
articles.description: Many artists treat SEO as a checklist of optimisation tricks. In reality, the real advantage comes from designing a creative process that produces work algorithms can recognise without compromising the ideas behind it.

articles.title: Why SEO Visibility Comes From Structural Legibility, Not Optimisation
articles.description: Many artists treat SEO as a checklist of optimisation tricks. In reality, the real advantage comes from designing a creative process that produces work algorithms can recognise without compromising the ideas behind it.

articles.title: Visibility Under Hostile Conditions
articles.description: Online discovery systems reward recognisable formats rather than meaning. For artists, this creates an environment where visibility is possible, but only under conditions that often distort the work itself.

generative-ai.title: AI SEO Strategy: Why Your Creative Work Is Invisible
generative-ai.description: This video case study shows how AI-era search changes the value of content. Instead of chasing keyword volume, the project uses semantic SEO, network analysis, suppressed and emerging nodes, and creative systems design to turn search results into a coherent creative operating system.

generative-ai.title: From SEO Keywords to the Haunted Machine: How AI Search Became a Creative Operating System
generative-ai.description: This video case study shows how AI-era search changes the value of content. Instead of chasing keyword volume, the project uses semantic SEO, network analysis, suppressed and emerging nodes, and creative systems design to turn search results into a coherent creative operating system.

generative-ai.title: Zombie Survival by ChatGPT — Why the AI Lies (and How to Stop It)
generative-ai.description: This video demonstrates how AI systems optimise for coherence rather than structural accuracy. SDA-3 reframes the model as a system to interrogate, forcing it to expose suppressed variables and recover constraint-compatible truth.

generative-ai.title: SDA-3 protocol
generative-ai.description: This post introduces SDA-3, a protocol for inferring the structure of an LLM’s embedding space through observable outputs, without relying on access to internal weights or hidden states.

generative-ai.title: Structural Extraction Protocol
generative-ai.description: A unified, procedural system for extracting structurally necessary logic from language model outputs through recursive constraint, adversarial interrogation, and collapse enforcement.

generative-ai.title: GPT Misuse Is Not What People Think
generative-ai.description: Most discussions about GPT misuse focus on malicious intent or careless users. In reality, misuse usually happens when people expect the model to do things it was never designed to do.

nlp.title: Python for NLP and Semantic SEO: A Practical Reference
nlp.description: A plain-language companion to the SEO Python codebase, explaining how the workflow uses search-result collection, NLP text processing, keyphrase extraction, embeddings, network analysis, and association rule mining for semantic SEO research.

instagram.title: Clustered Output (Dataset)
instagram.description: This post contains the structured cluster data derived from Instagram image analysis. It is presented in full without interpretation.

research-process.title: Summary of the Process, Conclusions, and Their Validity
research-process.description: This post presents a GPT-based analysis and evaluation of the research process used to investigate horror, surrealist, and symbolic aesthetics, assessing its conclusions for validity and internal consistency.

research-process.title: Research Process Used to Establish the Seer-Clown Archetype
research-process.description: This post outlines the structured, iterative research process used to develop the Seer-Clown archetype, combining data-driven analysis, artistic intuition, and philosophical exploration.

audience-resonance.title: Art Forms with High Audience Resonance
audience-resonance.description: This post documents a ChatGPT response generated during an analysis of datasets related to scraped SEO data.

audience-resonance.title: First Principles
audience-resonance.description: These are the first principles that guide my artwork. They emerged from my research into horror, psychology, symbolism, and audience response, and act as the underlying logic behind the work that appears on this site.

audience-resonance.title: Maximising Artistic Identity
audience-resonance.description: This post documents a ChatGPT response generated during an analysis of my artwork and related datasets. The goal of the exercise was to identify stylistic patterns and develop a clearer artistic direction.

audience-resonance.title: Most Popular Art Mediums Based on the Datasets
audience-resonance.description: This post documents a ChatGPT response generated during an analysis of statistical outputs from my SEO research process. The goal of the exercise was to identify which artistic mediums appear most frequently in audience-facing datasets and how those mediums might inform future production methods.

visibility.title: Visibility Under Hostile Conditions
visibility.description: Online discovery systems reward recognisable formats rather than meaning. For artists, this creates an environment where visibility is possible, but only under conditions that often distort the work itself.
